---
title: ""
description: "One source file. Every scientific output. A working blog by Dr Abdul Mannan."
listing:
  id: capability-posts
  contents: posts
  sort: "date desc"
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 3
  categories: true
  sort-ui: [date, title]
  filter-ui: true
  fields: [image, title, description, categories, date, reading-time]
  feed: true
page-layout: full
toc: false
anchor-sections: false
---

::: {.blog-hero}
::: {.blog-hero-inner}
[A reproducible publishing lab]{.blog-eyebrow}

# Knowledge has no limits. {.blog-title}

### Neither should our willingness to learn. {.blog-tagline}

From a badly aligned PowerPoint slide on a hospital laboratory computer in the
early 2000s — to coding my own books, reports, websites, dashboards and
scientific figures. This blog is that journey, and a **live demonstration of
everything [Quarto](https://quarto.org) can build from a single source file**.

[Read the story ↓](posts/2026-07-22-knowledge-has-no-limits/){.blog-cta}
&nbsp;&nbsp;
[Browse the gallery ↓](#capability-posts){.blog-cta .blog-cta-ghost}
:::
:::

::: {.blog-strip}
**Python** · **R** · **Julia** · **Observable JS** · **Pandoc** — one workflow that unifies analysis, narrative, references, figures and the final publication.
:::

## The gallery {#gallery}

Every card below is a **working example**. Each output type — from survival
curves to EPUB e-books that open in Apple Books — is built, rendered and
reproducible. Filter by category, or open a post to read the code that made it.
